why_safe_for_built_ux: Best single demand signal for underlying substitution because it reduces promo distortion.
notes: Prefer over Units as the primary modeled demand signal when available.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Observed real-world sales are intuitive and easy for business users to understand.
user_facing_role: Observed market outcome and business reality check.
notes: Best shown alongside Base Units and store-productivity measures rather than by itself.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Strong distribution context measure for separating reach changes from demand changes.
notes: Use to explain whether a gain or loss may be driven by distribution footprint.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Average price is intuitive and broadly understood by business stakeholders.
notes: Useful when interpreting switching driven by price differences.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Regular price context is useful when evaluating demand absent promo distortion.
notes: Only user-facing if sourced directly as a trusted SPINS measure, not a weak proxy.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Promo depth is intuitive and helps explain promotion-driven shifts.
notes: Use only if the clean Any Promo version is available.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Store count is intuitive and easy to explain in assortment and distribution conversations.
notes: Especially useful for explaining why sales movement may not be pure cannibalization. Use with Units per store measures to separate reach from demand.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Simple distribution percentage is intuitive for end users.
notes: Useful as a cleaner end-user counterpart to more technical reach metrics. Use with Units per store measures to separate penetration from true store-level demand change.

measure_name: Average Weekly Units Per Store Selling Per Item
why_safe_for_built_ux: Closest available SPINS measure to an intuitive units-per-store-selling-item view.
notes: Prefer this over Units/TDP or Units SPP for user-facing productivity views. Critical for separating true sales change from distribution change over time.

measure_name: Average Weekly Dollars Per Store Selling Per Item
why_safe_for_built_ux: Closest available SPINS measure to an intuitive dollars-per-store-selling-item view.
notes: Prefer this over Dollars/TDP or Dollars SPP for user-facing productivity views.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Launch timing is straightforward and critical for interpreting demand shifts.
notes: Helpful for explaining ramp, novelty effects, and post-launch substitution.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Subcategory share is a strong competitive-position measure close to the substitution set.
notes: Prefer subcategory share over broader category share for tighter cannibalization interpretation.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful but more technical and easier to misread without guidance.
notes: Best kept behind the scenes unless the audience is analytically mature.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Important analytically, but easy for end users to confuse with simple growth or weekly change.
notes: Keep as a modeling/support metric rather than a headline user-facing metric.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful for promo attribution but less intuitive than observed or base demand metrics.
notes: Better for internal diagnostics than user-facing output.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Helpful context but usually too operational for a polished demand story.
notes: Better as a drill-down or analyst-only field.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful in diagnostics but less elegant than Base Units for user-facing demand interpretation.
notes: Keep behind the scenes unless the workflow specifically centers on promo decomposition.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Operationally useful but not essential for a narrow polished experience.
notes: Better for analyst drill-down.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Helpful for diagnostics but not usually needed in the final UX.
notes: Better for analysis support than front-end storytelling.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful for decomposing promoted reach but more technical than standard TDP.
notes: Keep for diagnostics and root-cause analysis.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful but more technical than standard TDP for most business users.
notes: Keep for diagnostics and root-cause analysis.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful normalized productivity signal but not intuitive enough for headline UX.
notes: Prefer per-store-style measures for user-facing views.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful normalized productivity signal but not intuitive enough for headline UX.
notes: Prefer per-store-style measures for user-facing views.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Too easy to confuse with units-per-store even though it is ACV-point normalized.
user_facing_role: Fallback analyst proxy only if better store-based metrics are unavailable.
notes: Do not surface as a polished end-user productivity metric.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Too easy to confuse with dollars-per-store even though it is ACV-point normalized.
user_facing_role: Fallback analyst proxy only if better store-based metrics are unavailable.
notes: Do not surface as a polished end-user productivity metric.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Too easy to mistake for units per store when it is actually normalized by distribution points.
user_facing_role: Fallback analyst proxy only if better store-based metrics are unavailable.
notes: Do not use as a user-facing substitute for units per store.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Too easy to mistake for dollars per store when it is actually normalized by distribution points.
user_facing_role: Fallback analyst proxy only if better store-based metrics are unavailable.
notes: Do not use as a user-facing substitute for dollars per store.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful for diagnosis but secondary to simpler price and discount views.
notes: Keep as drill-down context.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful for diagnosis but secondary to simpler price and discount views.
notes: Keep as drill-down context.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Informative but technical and less intuitive than core demand and price measures.
notes: Better used in analyst diagnostics.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Broader than the most relevant substitution set and less precise than subcategory share.
notes: Prefer Unit Shr, Sub-Cat for the final UX.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Broader context measure that is less directly tied to the closest substitution set.
notes: Useful in diagnostics, not essential in final UX.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful share context but unit share is often easier to interpret in demand cannibalization discussions.
notes: Keep as analyst support unless revenue share is central to the story.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Observed dollar sales are intuitive and important for business decisions.
notes: Useful when the experience needs both demand and revenue framing.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Useful but usually secondary to Base Units in a demand-focused experience.
notes: Bring forward only if margin or revenue accretion is a major user need.

why_safe_for_built_ux: Helpful maturity context but less intuitive than First Week Selling.
notes: Best used behind the scenes or in metadata panels.
